message.content: In /home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/types.ts, find and return the full definition of the base `Node` interface — the one that all AST nodes extend. Also find the `NodeFlags` and `TransformFlags` enum definitions. Return the exact line numbers and full text of each. Search breadth: medium.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Now let me read these sections to get the full definitions:

content.content:
782	export const enum NodeFlags {
783	    None               = 0,
784	    Let                = 1 << 0,  // Variable declaration
785	    Const              = 1 << 1,  // Variable declaration
786	    Using              = 1 << 2,  // Variable declaration
787	    AwaitUsing         = Const | Using, // Variable declaration (NOTE: on a single node these flags would otherwise be mutually exclusive)
788	    NestedNamespace    = 1 << 3,  // Namespace declaration
789	    Synthesized        = 1 << 4,  // Node was synthesized during transformation
790	    Namespace          = 1 << 5,  // Namespace declaration
791	    OptionalChain      = 1 << 6,  // Chained MemberExpression rooted to a pseudo-OptionalExpression
792	    ExportContext      = 1 << 7,  // Export context (initialized by binding)
793	    ContainsThis       = 1 << 8,  // Interface contains references to "this"
794	    HasImplicitReturn  = 1 << 9,  // If function implicitly returns on one of codepaths (initialized by binding)
795	    HasExplicitReturn  = 1 << 10,  // If function has explicit reachable return on one of codepaths (initialized by binding)
796	    GlobalAugmentation = 1 << 11,  // Set if module declaration is an augmentation for the global scope
797	    HasAsyncFunctions  = 1 << 12, // If the file has async functions (initialized by binding)
798	    DisallowInContext  = 1 << 13, // If node was parsed in a context where 'in-expressions' are not allowed
799	    YieldContext       = 1 << 14, // If node was parsed in the 'yield' context created when parsing a generator
800	    DecoratorContext   = 1 << 15, // If node was parsed as part of a decorator
801	    AwaitContext       = 1 << 16, // If node was parsed in the 'await' context created when parsing an async function
802	    DisallowConditionalTypesContext = 1 << 17, // If node was parsed in a context where conditional types are not allowed
803	    ThisNodeHasError   = 1 << 18, // If the parser encountered an error when parsing the code that created this node
804	    JavaScriptFile     = 1 << 19, // If node was parsed in a JavaScript
805	    ThisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError = 1 << 20, // If this node or any of its children had an error
806	    HasAggregatedChildData = 1 << 21, // If we've computed data from children and cached it in this node
807	
808	    // These flags will be set when the parser encounters a dynamic import expression or 'import.meta' to avoid
809	    // walking the tree if the flags are not set. However, these flags are just a approximation
810	    // (hence why it's named "PossiblyContainsDynamicImport") because once set, the flags never get cleared.
811	    // During editing, if a dynamic import is removed, incremental parsing will *NOT* clear this flag.
812	    // This means that the tree will always be traversed during module resolution, or when looking for external module indicators.
813	    // However, the removal operation should not occur often and in the case of the
814	    // removal, it is likely that users will add the import anyway.
815	    // The advantage of this approach is its simplicity. For the case of batch compilation,
816	    // we guarantee that users won't have to pay the price of walking the tree if a dynamic import isn't used.
817	    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsDynamicImport = 1 << 22,
818	    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsImportMeta    = 1 << 23,
819	
820	    JSDoc                                          = 1 << 24, // If node was parsed inside jsdoc
821	    /** @internal */ Ambient                       = 1 << 25, // If node was inside an ambient context -- a declaration file, or inside something with the `declare` modifier.
822	    /** @internal */ InWithStatement               = 1 << 26, // If any ancestor of node was the `statement` of a WithStatement (not the `expression`)
823	    JsonFile                                       = 1 << 27, // If node was parsed in a Json
824	    /** @internal */ TypeCached                    = 1 << 28, // If a type was cached for node at any point
825	    /** @internal */ Deprecated                    = 1 << 29, // If has '@deprecated' JSDoc tag
826	    /** @internal */ Unreachable                   = 1 << 30, // If node is unreachable according to the binder
827	
828	    BlockScoped = Let | Const | Using,
829	    Constant = Const | Using,
830	
831	    ReachabilityCheckFlags = HasImplicitReturn | HasExplicitReturn,
832	    ReachabilityAndEmitFlags = ReachabilityCheckFlags | HasAsyncFunctions,
833	
834	    // Parsing context flags
835	    ContextFlags = DisallowInContext | DisallowConditionalTypesContext | YieldContext | DecoratorContext | AwaitContext | JavaScriptFile | InWithStatement | Ambient,
836	
837	    // Exclude these flags when parsing a Type
838	    TypeExcludesFlags = YieldContext | AwaitContext,
839	
840	    // Represents all flags that are potentially set once and
841	    // never cleared on SourceFiles which get re-used in between incremental parses.
842	    // See the comment above on `PossiblyContainsDynamicImport` and `PossiblyContainsImportMeta`.
843	    /** @internal */ PermanentlySetIncrementalFlags = PossiblyContainsDynamicImport | PossiblyContainsImportMeta,
844	
845	    // The following flags repurpose other NodeFlags as different meanings for Identifier nodes
846	    /** @internal */ IdentifierHasExtendedUnicodeEscape = ContainsThis, // Indicates whether the identifier contains an extended unicode escape sequence
847	    /** @internal */ IdentifierIsInJSDocNamespace = HasAsyncFunctions, // Indicates whether the identifier is part of a JSDoc namespace
848	}
849	
850	// dprint-ignore
851	export const enum ModifierFlags {
852	    None =               0,
853	
854	    // Syntactic/JSDoc modifiers
855	    Public =             1 << 0,  // Property/Method
856	    Private =            1 << 1,  // Property/Method
857	    Protected =          1 << 2,  // Property/Method
858	    Readonly =           1 << 3,  // Property/Method
859	    Override =           1 << 4,  // Override method.
860	
861	    // Syntactic-only modifiers
862	    Export =             1 << 5,  // Declarations
863	    Abstract =           1 << 6,  // Class/Method/ConstructSignature
864	    Ambient =            1 << 7,  // Declarations
865	    Static =             1 << 8,  // Property/Method
866	    Accessor =           1 << 9,  // Property
867	    Async =              1 << 10, // Property/Method/Function
868	    Default =            1 << 11, // Function/Class (export default declaration)
869	    Const =              1 << 12, // Const enum
870	    In =                 1 << 13, // Contravariance modifier
871	    Out =                1 << 14, // Covariance modifier
872	    Decorator =          1 << 15, // Contains a decorator.
873	
874	    // JSDoc-only modifiers
875	    Deprecated =         1 << 16, // Deprecated tag.
876	
877	    // Cache-only JSDoc-modifiers. Should match order of Syntactic/JSDoc modifiers, above.
878	    /** @internal */ JSDocPublic = 1 << 23, // if this value changes, `selectEffectiveModifierFlags` must change accordingly
879	    /** @internal */ JSDocPrivate = 1 << 24,
880	    /** @internal */ JSDocProtected = 1 << 25,
881	    /** @internal */ JSDocReadonly = 1 << 26,
882	    /** @internal */ JSDocOverride = 1 << 27,
883	
884	    /** @internal */ SyntacticOrJSDocModifiers = Public | Private | Protected | Readonly | Override,
885	    /** @internal */ SyntacticOnlyModifiers = Export | Ambient | Abstract | Static | Accessor | Async | Default | Const | In | Out | Decorator,
886	    /** @internal */ SyntacticModifiers = SyntacticOrJSDocModifiers | SyntacticOnlyModifiers,
887	    /** @internal */ JSDocCacheOnlyModifiers = JSDocPublic | JSDocPrivate | JSDocProtected | JSDocReadonly | JSDocOverride,
888	    /** @internal */ JSDocOnlyModifiers = Deprecated,
889	    /** @internal */ NonCacheOnlyModifiers = SyntacticOrJSDocModifiers | SyntacticOnlyModifiers | JSDocOnlyModifiers,
890	
891	    HasComputedJSDocModifiers = 1 << 28, // Indicates the computed modifier flags include modifiers from JSDoc.
892	    HasComputedFlags =   1 << 29, // Modifier flags have been computed
893	
894	    AccessibilityModifier = Public | Private | Protected,
895	    // Accessibility modifiers and 'readonly' can be attached to a parameter in a constructor to make it a property.
896	    ParameterPropertyModifier = AccessibilityModifier | Readonly | Override,
897	    NonPublicAccessibilityModifier = Private | Protected,
898	
899	    TypeScriptModifier = Ambient | Public | Private | Protected | Readonly | Abstract | Const | Override | In | Out,
900	    ExportDefault = Export | Default,
901	    All = Export | Ambient | Public | Private | Protected | Static | Readonly | Abstract | Accessor | Async | Default | Const | Deprecated | Override | In | Out | Decorator,
902	    Modifier = All & ~Decorator,
903	}
904	
905	export const enum JsxFlags {
906	    None = 0,
907	    /** An element from a named property of the JSX.IntrinsicElements interface */
908	    IntrinsicNamedElement = 1 << 0,
909	    /** An element inferred from the string index signature of the JSX.IntrinsicElements interface */
910	    IntrinsicIndexedElement = 1 << 1,
911	
912	    IntrinsicElement = IntrinsicNamedElement | IntrinsicIndexedElement,
913	}
914	
915	// dprint-ignore
916	/** @internal */
917	export const enum RelationComparisonResult {
918	    None                = 0,
919	    Succeeded           = 1 << 0, // Should be truthy
920	    Failed              = 1 << 1,
921	
922	    ReportsUnmeasurable = 1 << 3,
923	    ReportsUnreliable   = 1 << 4,
924	    ReportsMask         = ReportsUnmeasurable | ReportsUnreliable,
925	
926	    ComplexityOverflow  = 1 << 5,
927	    StackDepthOverflow  = 1 << 6,
928	    Overflow            = ComplexityOverflow | StackDepthOverflow,
929	}
930	
931	/** @internal */

942	export interface Node extends ReadonlyTextRange {
943	    readonly kind: SyntaxKind;
944	    readonly flags: NodeFlags;
945	    /** @internal */ modifierFlagsCache: ModifierFlags;
946	    /** @internal */ readonly transformFlags: TransformFlags; // Flags for transforms
947	    /** @internal */ id?: NodeId; // Unique id (used to look up NodeLinks)
948	    readonly parent: Node; // Parent node (initialized by binding)
949	    /** @internal */ original?: Node; // The original node if this is an updated node.
950	    /** @internal */ emitNode?: EmitNode; // Associated EmitNode (initialized by transforms)
951	    // NOTE: `symbol` and `localSymbol` have been moved to `Declaration`
952	    //       `locals` and `nextContainer` have been moved to `LocalsContainer`
953	    //       `flowNode` has been moved to `FlowContainer`
954	    //       see: https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/pull/51682
955	}
956	
957	export interface JSDocContainer extends Node {
958	    _jsdocContainerBrand: any;
959	    /** @internal */ jsDoc?: JSDocArray; // JSDoc that directly precedes this node
960	}
961	
962	/** @internal */
963	export interface JSDocArray extends Array<JSDoc> {
964	    jsDocCache?: readonly JSDocTag[]; // Cache for getJSDocTags
965	}
966	
967	export interface LocalsContainer extends Node {
968	    _localsContainerBrand: any;
969	    /** @internal */ locals?: SymbolTable; // Locals associated with node (initialized by binding)
970	    /** @internal */ nextContainer?: HasLocals; // Next container in declaration order (initialized by binding)
971	}
972	
973	export interface FlowContainer extends Node {
974	    _flowContainerBrand: any;
975	    /** @internal */ flowNode?: FlowNode; // Associated FlowNode (initialized by binding)
976	}
977	
978	/** @internal */
979	export type HasFlowNode =
980	    | Identifier
981	    | ThisExpression
982	    | SuperExpression
983	    | QualifiedName
984	    | MetaProperty
985	    | ElementAccessExpression
986	    | PropertyAccessExpression
987	    | BindingElement
988	    | FunctionExpression
989	    | ArrowFunction
990	    | MethodDeclaration
991	    | GetAccessorDeclaration
992	    | SetAccessorDeclaration
993	    | VariableStatement
994	    | ExpressionStatement
995	    | IfStatement
996	    | DoStatement
997	    | WhileStatement
998	    | ForStatement
999	    | ForInStatement
1000	    | ForOfStatement
1001	    | ContinueStatement
1002	    | BreakStatement
1003	    | ReturnStatement
1004	    | WithStatement
1005	    | SwitchStatement
1006	    | LabeledStatement
1007	    | ThrowStatement
1008	    | TryStatement
1009	    | DebuggerStatement;
1010	
1011	// Ideally, `ForEachChildNodes` and `VisitEachChildNodes` would not differ.
1012	// However, `forEachChild` currently processes JSDoc comment syntax and missing declarations more thoroughly.
1013	// On the other hand, `visitEachChild` actually processes `Identifier`s (which really *shouldn't* have children,
1014	// but are constructed as if they could for faked-up `QualifiedName`s in the language service.)
1015	
1016	/** @internal */
1017	export type ForEachChildNodes =
1018	    | HasChildren
1019	    | MissingDeclaration
1020	    | JSDocTypeExpression
1021	    | JSDocNonNullableType
1022	    | JSDocNullableType
1023	    | JSDocOptionalType
1024	    | JSDocVariadicType
1025	    | JSDocFunctionType
1026	    | JSDoc
1027	    | JSDocSeeTag
1028	    | JSDocNameReference
1029	    | JSDocMemberName
1030	    | JSDocParameterTag
1031	    | JSDocPropertyTag
1032	    | JSDocAuthorTag
1033	    | JSDocImplementsTag
1034	    | JSDocAugmentsTag
1035	    | JSDocTemplateTag
1036	    | JSDocTypedefTag
1037	    | JSDocCallbackTag
1038	    | JSDocReturnTag
1039	    | JSDocTypeTag
1040	    | JSDocThisTag
1041	    | JSDocEnumTag

8212	export const enum TransformFlags {
8213	    None = 0,
8214	
8215	    // Facts
8216	    // - Flags used to indicate that a node or subtree contains syntax that requires transformation.
8217	    ContainsTypeScript = 1 << 0,
8218	    ContainsJsx = 1 << 1,
8219	    ContainsESNext = 1 << 2,
8220	    ContainsES2022 = 1 << 3,
8221	    ContainsES2021 = 1 << 4,
8222	    ContainsES2020 = 1 << 5,
8223	    ContainsES2019 = 1 << 6,
8224	    ContainsES2018 = 1 << 7,
8225	    ContainsES2017 = 1 << 8,
8226	    ContainsES2016 = 1 << 9,
8227	    ContainsES2015 = 1 << 10,
8228	    ContainsGenerator = 1 << 11,
8229	    ContainsDestructuringAssignment = 1 << 12,
8230	
8231	    // Markers
8232	    // - Flags used to indicate that a subtree contains a specific transformation.
8233	    ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax = 1 << 13, // Property Initializers, Parameter Property Initializers
8234	    ContainsLexicalThis = 1 << 14,
8235	    ContainsRestOrSpread = 1 << 15,
8236	    ContainsObjectRestOrSpread = 1 << 16,
8237	    ContainsComputedPropertyName = 1 << 17,
8238	    ContainsBlockScopedBinding = 1 << 18,
8239	    ContainsBindingPattern = 1 << 19,
8240	    ContainsYield = 1 << 20,
8241	    ContainsAwait = 1 << 21,
8242	    ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion = 1 << 22,
8243	    ContainsDynamicImport = 1 << 23,
8244	    ContainsClassFields = 1 << 24,
8245	    ContainsDecorators = 1 << 25,
8246	    ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait = 1 << 26,
8247	    ContainsLexicalSuper = 1 << 27,
8248	    ContainsUpdateExpressionForIdentifier = 1 << 28,
8249	    ContainsPrivateIdentifierInExpression = 1 << 29,
8250	
8251	    HasComputedFlags = 1 << 31, // Transform flags have been computed.
8252	
8253	    // Assertions
8254	    // - Bitmasks that are used to assert facts about the syntax of a node and its subtree.
8255	    AssertTypeScript = ContainsTypeScript,
8256	    AssertJsx = ContainsJsx,
8257	    AssertESNext = ContainsESNext,
8258	    AssertES2022 = ContainsES2022,
8259	    AssertES2021 = ContainsES2021,
8260	    AssertES2020 = ContainsES2020,
8261	    AssertES2019 = ContainsES2019,
8262	    AssertES2018 = ContainsES2018,
8263	    AssertES2017 = ContainsES2017,
8264	    AssertES2016 = ContainsES2016,
8265	    AssertES2015 = ContainsES2015,
8266	    AssertGenerator = ContainsGenerator,
8267	    AssertDestructuringAssignment = ContainsDestructuringAssignment,
8268	
8269	    // Scope Exclusions
8270	    // - Bitmasks that exclude flags from propagating out of a specific context
8271	    //   into the subtree flags of their container.
8272	    OuterExpressionExcludes = HasComputedFlags,
8273	    PropertyAccessExcludes = OuterExpressionExcludes,
8274	    NodeExcludes = PropertyAccessExcludes,
8275	    ArrowFunctionExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax | ContainsBlockScopedBinding | ContainsYield | ContainsAwait | ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion | ContainsBindingPattern | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread | ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait,
8276	    FunctionExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax | ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper | ContainsBlockScopedBinding | ContainsYield | ContainsAwait | ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion | ContainsBindingPattern | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread | ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait,
8277	    ConstructorExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper | ContainsBlockScopedBinding | ContainsYield | ContainsAwait | ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion | ContainsBindingPattern | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread | ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait,
8278	    MethodOrAccessorExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper | ContainsBlockScopedBinding | ContainsYield | ContainsAwait | ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion | ContainsBindingPattern | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread,
8279	    PropertyExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper,
8280	    ClassExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax | ContainsComputedPropertyName,
8281	    ModuleExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax | ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper | ContainsBlockScopedBinding | ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion | ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait,
8282	    TypeExcludes = ~ContainsTypeScript,
8283	    ObjectLiteralExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax | ContainsComputedPropertyName | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread,
8284	    ArrayLiteralOrCallOrNewExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsRestOrSpread,
8285	    VariableDeclarationListExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsBindingPattern | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread,
8286	    ParameterExcludes = NodeExcludes,
8287	    CatchClauseExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread,
8288	    BindingPatternExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsRestOrSpread,
8289	    ContainsLexicalThisOrSuper = ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper,
8290	
8291	    // Propagating flags
8292	    // - Bitmasks for flags that should propagate from a child
8293	    PropertyNamePropagatingFlags = ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper,
8294	    // Masks
8295	    // - Additional bitmasks
8296	}
8297	
8298	export interface SourceMapRange extends TextRange {
8299	    source?: SourceMapSource;
8300	}
8301	
8302	export interface SourceMapSource {
8303	    fileName: string;
8304	    text: string;
8305	    /** @internal */ lineMap: readonly number[];
8306	    skipTrivia?: (pos: number) => number;
8307	}
8308	
8309	/** @internal */
8310	// NOTE: Any new properties should be accounted for in `mergeEmitNode` in factory/nodeFactory.ts

content.text:
Perfect! I have found all three definitions. Let me provide you with the complete information:

## Findings

### 1. **NodeFlags enum** (Lines 782-848)
**Location:** `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/types.ts:782-848`

```typescript
export const enum NodeFlags {
    None               = 0,
    Let                = 1 << 0,  // Variable declaration
    Const              = 1 << 1,  // Variable declaration
    Using              = 1 << 2,  // Variable declaration
    AwaitUsing         = Const | Using, // Variable declaration (NOTE: on a single node these flags would otherwise be mutually exclusive)
    NestedNamespace    = 1 << 3,  // Namespace declaration
    Synthesized        = 1 << 4,  // Node was synthesized during transformation
    Namespace          = 1 << 5,  // Namespace declaration
    OptionalChain      = 1 << 6,  // Chained MemberExpression rooted to a pseudo-OptionalExpression
    ExportContext      = 1 << 7,  // Export context (initialized by binding)
    ContainsThis       = 1 << 8,  // Interface contains references to "this"
    HasImplicitReturn  = 1 << 9,  // If function implicitly returns on one of codepaths (initialized by binding)
    HasExplicitReturn  = 1 << 10,  // If function has explicit reachable return on one of codepaths (initialized by binding)
    GlobalAugmentation = 1 << 11,  // Set if module declaration is an augmentation for the global scope
    HasAsyncFunctions  = 1 << 12, // If the file has async functions (initialized by binding)
    DisallowInContext  = 1 << 13, // If node was parsed in a context where 'in-expressions' are not allowed
    YieldContext       = 1 << 14, // If node was parsed in the 'yield' context created when parsing a generator
    DecoratorContext   = 1 << 15, // If node was parsed as part of a decorator
    AwaitContext       = 1 << 16, // If node was parsed in the 'await' context created when parsing an async function
    DisallowConditionalTypesContext = 1 << 17, // If node was parsed in a context where conditional types are not allowed
    ThisNodeHasError   = 1 << 18, // If the parser encountered an error when parsing the code that created this node
    JavaScriptFile     = 1 << 19, // If node was parsed in a JavaScript
    ThisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError = 1 << 20, // If this node or any of its children had an error
    HasAggregatedChildData = 1 << 21, // If we've computed data from children and cached it in this node

    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsDynamicImport = 1 << 22,
    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsImportMeta    = 1 << 23,

    JSDoc                                          = 1 << 24, // If node was parsed inside jsdoc
    /** @internal */ Ambient                       = 1 << 25, // If node was inside an ambient context -- a declaration file, or inside something with the `declare` modifier.
    /** @internal */ InWithStatement               = 1 << 26, // If any ancestor of node was the `statement` of a WithStatement (not the `expression`)
    JsonFile                                       = 1 << 27, // If node was parsed in a Json
    /** @internal */ TypeCached                    = 1 << 28, // If a type was cached for node at any point
    /** @internal */ Deprecated                    = 1 << 29, // If has '@deprecated' JSDoc tag
    /** @internal */ Unreachable                   = 1 << 30, // If node is unreachable according to the binder

    BlockScoped = Let | Const | Using,
    Constant = Const | Using,

    ReachabilityCheckFlags = HasImplicitReturn | HasExplicitReturn,
    ReachabilityAndEmitFlags = ReachabilityCheckFlags | HasAsyncFunctions,

    // Parsing context flags
    ContextFlags = DisallowInContext | DisallowConditionalTypesContext | YieldContext | DecoratorContext | AwaitContext | JavaScriptFile | InWithStatement | Ambient,

    // Exclude these flags when parsing a Type
    TypeExcludesFlags = YieldContext | AwaitContext,

    /** @internal */ PermanentlySetIncrementalFlags = PossiblyContainsDynamicImport | PossiblyContainsImportMeta,

    // The following flags repurpose other NodeFlags as different meanings for Identifier nodes
    /** @internal */ IdentifierHasExtendedUnicodeEscape = ContainsThis,
    /** @internal */ IdentifierIsInJSDocNamespace = HasAsyncFunctions,
}
```

### 2. **Node interface** (Lines 942-955)
**Location:** `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/types.ts:942-955`

```typescript
export interface Node extends ReadonlyTextRange {
    readonly kind: SyntaxKind;
    readonly flags: NodeFlags;
    /** @internal */ modifierFlagsCache: ModifierFlags;
    /** @internal */ readonly transformFlags: TransformFlags; // Flags for transforms
    /** @internal */ id?: NodeId; // Unique id (used to look up NodeLinks)
    readonly parent: Node; // Parent node (initialized by binding)
    /** @internal */ original?: Node; // The original node if this is an updated node.
    /** @internal */ emitNode?: EmitNode; // Associated EmitNode (initialized by transforms)
    // NOTE: `symbol` and `localSymbol` have been moved to `Declaration`
    //       `locals` and `nextContainer` have been moved to `LocalsContainer`
    //       `flowNode` has been moved to `FlowContainer`
    //       see: https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/pull/51682
}
```

### 3. **TransformFlags enum** (Lines 8212-8296)
**Location:** `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/types.ts:8212-8296`

```typescript
export const enum TransformFlags {
    None = 0,

    // Facts
    // - Flags used to indicate that a node or subtree contains syntax that requires transformation.
    ContainsTypeScript = 1 << 0,
    ContainsJsx = 1 << 1,
    ContainsESNext = 1 << 2,
    ContainsES2022 = 1 << 3,
    ContainsES2021 = 1 << 4,
    ContainsES2020 = 1 << 5,
    ContainsES2019 = 1 << 6,
    ContainsES2018 = 1 << 7,
    ContainsES2017 = 1 << 8,
    ContainsES2016 = 1 << 9,
    ContainsES2015 = 1 << 10,
    ContainsGenerator = 1 << 11,
    ContainsDestructuringAssignment = 1 << 12,

    // Markers
    // - Flags used to indicate that a subtree contains a specific transformation.
    ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax = 1 << 13, // Property Initializers, Parameter Property Initializers
    ContainsLexicalThis = 1 << 14,
    ContainsRestOrSpread = 1 << 15,
    ContainsObjectRestOrSpread = 1 << 16,
    ContainsComputedPropertyName = 1 << 17,
    ContainsBlockScopedBinding = 1 << 18,
    ContainsBindingPattern = 1 << 19,
    ContainsYield = 1 << 20,
    ContainsAwait = 1 << 21,
    ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion = 1 << 22,
    ContainsDynamicImport = 1 << 23,
    ContainsClassFields = 1 << 24,
    ContainsDecorators = 1 << 25,
    ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait = 1 << 26,
    ContainsLexicalSuper = 1 << 27,
    ContainsUpdateExpressionForIdentifier = 1 << 28,
    ContainsPrivateIdentifierInExpression = 1 << 29,

    HasComputedFlags = 1 << 31, // Transform flags have been computed.

    // Assertions
    // - Bitmasks that are used to assert facts about the syntax of a node and its subtree.
    AssertTypeScript = ContainsTypeScript,
    AssertJsx = ContainsJsx,
    AssertESNext = ContainsESNext,
    AssertES2022 = ContainsES2022,
    AssertES2021 = ContainsES2021,
    AssertES2020 = ContainsES2020,
    AssertES2019 = ContainsES2019,
    AssertES2018 = ContainsES2018,
    AssertES2017 = ContainsES2017,
    AssertES2016 = ContainsES2016,
    AssertES2015 = ContainsES2015,
    AssertGenerator = ContainsGenerator,
    AssertDestructuringAssignment = ContainsDestructuringAssignment,

    // Scope Exclusions
    // - Bitmasks that exclude flags from propagating out of a specific context
    //   into the subtree flags of their container.
    OuterExpressionExcludes = HasComputedFlags,
    PropertyAccessExcludes = OuterExpressionExcludes,
    NodeExcludes = PropertyAccessExcludes,
    ArrowFunctionExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax | ContainsBlockScopedBinding | ContainsYield | ContainsAwait | ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion | ContainsBindingPattern | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread | ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait,
    FunctionExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax | ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper | ContainsBlockScopedBinding | ContainsYield | ContainsAwait | ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion | ContainsBindingPattern | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread | ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait,
    ConstructorExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper | ContainsBlockScopedBinding | ContainsYield | ContainsAwait | ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion | ContainsBindingPattern | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread | ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait,
    MethodOrAccessorExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper | ContainsBlockScopedBinding | ContainsYield | ContainsAwait | ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion | ContainsBindingPattern | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread,
    PropertyExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper,
    ClassExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax | ContainsComputedPropertyName,
    ModuleExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax | ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper | ContainsBlockScopedBinding | ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion | ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait,
    TypeExcludes = ~ContainsTypeScript,
    ObjectLiteralExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax | ContainsComputedPropertyName | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread,
    ArrayLiteralOrCallOrNewExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsRestOrSpread,
    VariableDeclarationListExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsBindingPattern | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread,
    ParameterExcludes = NodeExcludes,
    CatchClauseExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsObjectRestOrSpread,
    BindingPatternExcludes = NodeExcludes | ContainsRestOrSpread,
    ContainsLexicalThisOrSuper = ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper,

    // Propagating flags
    // - Bitmasks for flags that should propagate from a child
    PropertyNamePropagatingFlags = ContainsLexicalThis | ContainsLexicalSuper,
}
```
